title: tests: stabilize e2e metrics assertions
body:
# Description

This change fixes the flakiness in the metrics e2e test reported in #9710.

The failure was caused by the test scraping the metrics endpoint once and immediately asserting on counters that are only eventually visible. When the scrape happened before the expected samples were exposed, the test saw a zero value and failed even though the runtime behavior was correct.

The fix keeps the change local to the existing metrics e2e test and makes the assertions wait for the expected metrics to appear:

- HTTP metrics now poll until the expected `GET` and `POST` samples for the target app are present with non-zero counts.
- gRPC metrics now poll until the `SaveState` counter reaches the expected value.
- The metrics-disabled case now polls and confirms that the HTTP server metric never appears for that app.
- Metric parsing was tightened so the test matches samples for the specific app under test instead of relying on broader assumptions.

This keeps the test aligned with the way Dapr's newer metrics integration tests already handle metric visibility, while avoiding changes outside the affected e2e test.

## Issue reference

Please reference the issue this PR will close: #9710

## Checklist

- [x] Code compiles correctly
- [x] Created/updated tests
- [ ] Unit tests passing
- [ ] End-to-end tests passing
- [ ] Extended the documentation / Created issue in the <https://github.com/dapr/docs/> repo: dapr/docs#_[issue number]_
- [ ] Specification has been updated / Created issue in the <https://github.com/dapr/docs/> repo: dapr/docs#_[issue number]_
- [ ] Provided sample for the feature / Created issue in the <https://github.com/dapr/docs/> repo: dapr/docs#_[issue number]_

## Verification

The following checks were run successfully in this workspace:

- `gofmt -w tests/e2e/metrics/metrics_test.go`
- `go vet -tags=e2e ./tests/e2e/metrics`
- `go test -c -tags=e2e ./tests/e2e/metrics`
- `go test -c -o /tmp/metrics-e2e.test -tags=e2e ./tests/e2e/metrics`
- `go test ./tests/integration/suite/daprd/metrics/... -run TestDoesNotExist -count=1`
- `git diff --check`

The full Kubernetes e2e suite could not be run from this environment because the configured cluster endpoint was unavailable.
